title: Offenses and Penalties under the 2016 Rules Seabased
content: This section enumerates various offenses related to conduct and discipline of seafarers, including gambling, violation of company policies, incompetence, inciting mutiny, grave abuse of authority, gross misbehavior, negligence, false statements, safety violations, drug and alcohol policy violations, failure to refund repatriation costs, and violation of the Code of Discipline for Seafarers. Each offense is associated with graduated penalties ranging from suspension from participation in the overseas employment program (with durations varying from six months to three years) to permanent disqualification from participation in the overseas employment program. Specific offenses include but are not limited to: gambling that disrupts harmony or is not purely recreational; pilferage or theft of ship's property or cargo; embezzlement; unauthorized disposal of company property; dishonesty; gross negligence; failure to observe regulations; disorderly conduct; immorality; inflicting harm; incompetence; inciting mutiny; concerted breach of contracts; grave abuse of authority; gross misbehavior; gross negligence causing damage; connivance with stowaways; false statements; casting aspersions on the company; safety and environmental violations; drug and alcohol policy violations; failure to refund repatriation costs; and violation of the Code of Discipline for Seafarers. Penalties escalate with repeated offenses, emphasizing the importance of maintaining discipline and good order aboard ships and in overseas employment programs.
